data.title: Is Hormel Foods (HRL) Recasting Its Retail Strategy With Domenic Borrelli’s Appointment?
data.description:
• Hormel Foods has appointed Domenic Borrelli as executive vice president of Retail, effective February 23, 2026, placing him in charge of one of the company’s largest consumer-brand business units.
  • By bringing in a leader with extensive experience in brand building and complex business transformations at Danone, Kraft Foods and Maple Leaf Foods, Hormel Foods is signaling a renewed focus on reshaping its retail portfolio and execution.
  • We’ll now examine how bringing in Domenic Borrelli to lead Hormel’s Retail unit may influence the company’s broader investment narrative.

Hormel Foods Investment Narrative Recap

To own Hormel Foods, you need to believe in the resilience of its branded retail portfolio as it works through margin pressure from higher input costs and slower revenue growth. The appointment of Domenic Borrelli to lead the Retail unit does not immediately change the near term focus on rebuilding profitability or the key risk around persistent commodity inflation and the lag in passing through higher costs to consumers.

The most relevant recent development alongside Borrelli’s hire is Hormel’s 2026 guidance, which calls for US$12.2 billion to US$12.5 billion in net sales and US$0.96 billion to US$1.03 billion in operating income. Retail leadership execution will be central to how the company tracks against these targets, particularly as it balances pricing, mix and innovation against evolving consumer preferences for alternative and clean label proteins.

However, investors should also be aware of the risk that input cost volatility and slower pricing pass through could...

data.title: A Look At Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO) Valuation After Recent Analyst Caution On Macau Gaming Prospects
data.description:
Recent analyst actions around Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO) have turned more cautious, with JPMorgan moving to Neutral and BofA signaling a selective stance as they flag sector wide Macau gaming risks and margin concerns.

See our latest analysis for Melco Resorts & Entertainment.

At a share price of $6.73, Melco’s recent 30 day share price return of 14.59% and year to date share price return of 10.39% contrast with a 1 year total shareholder return of 23.49%. The 3 and 5 year total shareholder returns of 48.31% and 60.13% highlight how earlier losses still weigh on long term performance and suggest that recent analyst caution around Macau and Melco’s margin outlook is arriving just as shorter term momentum has been fading again.

If analyst downgrades and sector wide Macau questions have you reassessing your watchlist, it could be a useful moment to look across aerospace and defense stocks as a very different corner of the market.

With MLCO trading at $6.73, a value score of 4 and a wide gap to the average analyst target, investors have to ask whether this discount points to mispricing or if the market is already bracing for weaker growth.

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 25.2x: Is it justified?

At a last close of $6.73, Melco Resorts & Entertainment trades on a P/E of 25.2x, which analysts flag as good value versus some peers but expensive against the broader US Hospitality group.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price with earnings per share, so it effectively shows how much investors are paying for each dollar of current profit. For a casino and resort operator like Melco, where profitability has only recently returned, this multiple can also reflect how the market weighs fresh earnings against a long stretch of earlier losses.

Our data suggests the current P/E sits below an estimated fair P/E of 27.1x. This signals the market is placing a slightly lower value on Melco’s earnings than the level implied by the SWS fair ratio model. That fair ratio level is a reference point the valuation could gravitate toward if sentiment and fundamentals stay aligned with the framework behind that estimate.

Compared with its direct peer group, Melco’s 25.2x P/E screens as good value versus a peer average of 40.1x. However, it is still described as expensive against the US Hospitality industry average of 21.4x. That split paints a picture of a stock priced richer than the wider sector, but cheaper than closer comparables, leaving room for readers to judge whether the peer premium or the sector discount carries more weight.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Melco Resorts & Entertainment

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 25.2x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, shorter term share price declines, along with questions around Macau related revenue and margin pressure, could quickly challenge any case built on valuation support alone.

Find out about the key risks to this Melco Resorts & Entertainment narrative.

Another View Using Our DCF Model

The SWS DCF model paints a very different picture. With an estimate of fair value at $21.61 versus the current $6.73 share price, it suggests the stock is trading at a heavy discount. If the P/E looks about right but the DCF points much higher, which signal do you trust more?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 868 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

data.title: Did TransUnion’s (TRU) AI-Powered Win-Back Results Just Shift Its Data Monetization Narrative?
data.description:
• Earlier this month, TransUnion and Actable reported that integrating TransUnion’s TruAudience Marketing Solutions data into Actable’s AI models improved marketing prediction accuracy and cut false positives for a major retailer’s customer win-back campaign.
  • The collaboration highlights how TransUnion’s extensive identity graph and enrichment capabilities can fill data gaps in costly, data-scarce marketing use cases such as win-back, prospecting, and luxury categories.
  • Now we’ll assess how this AI-driven uplift in marketing model performance could influence TransUnion’s investment narrative and long-term growth drivers.

TransUnion Investment Narrative Recap

To own TransUnion, you need to believe its data and analytics can stay essential as credit, marketing, and fraud decisions become more AI driven. The Actable results reinforce the AI and analytics catalyst in the near term, but do not materially change the biggest risk right now: ongoing regulatory and privacy pressures on how data is collected, linked, and monetized.

The recent hire of Francesca Noli to lead Global Consumer Solutions is particularly relevant here, because it underlines TransUnion’s push to turn its data and AI capabilities into more consumer-facing products like TruEmpower. If management executes, this could complement TruAudience and identity/fraud solutions as key earnings growth drivers, even as investors watch for rising compliance and modernization costs.

Yet investors should also be aware that tighter data privacy rules or a major cyber incident could quickly change how sustainably this model scales...

data.title: How Qnity Electronics’ CFO Transition And Reaffirmed 2025 Guidance At Q (Q) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Qnity Electronics, Inc. recently announced that Chief Financial Officer Matt Harbaugh has stepped down for health reasons, appointing current Principal Accounting Officer and Controller Mike Goss as Interim CFO while it conducts a comprehensive search for a permanent successor and reaffirming its full-year 2025 net sales guidance of approximately US$4.7 billion.
  • The choice of an interim CFO with nearly three decades of finance experience, including senior roles at DuPont and Ernst & Young, underscores Qnity’s emphasis on continuity in financial oversight during this leadership transition.
  • We’ll now examine how reaffirmed guidance alongside Mike Goss’s appointment as interim CFO shapes Qnity Electronics’ broader investment narrative.

Outshine the giants: these 24 early-stage AI stocks could fund your retirement.

Advertisement

What Is Qnity Electronics' Investment Narrative?

For Qnity Electronics, the core investment case still comes down to believing in a focused, stand-alone electronics materials business that can convert its US$4.7 billion net sales outlook into resilient, high-quality earnings. The recent CFO change introduces a governance wrinkle just months after the DuPont spin-off, but the board’s decision to appoint long-time Controller and Principal Accounting Officer Mike Goss as interim CFO, while reaffirming 2025 guidance, suggests limited immediate disruption to near-term catalysts such as index inclusion flows, execution on long-term supply agreements and early capital allocation decisions. The bigger risks remain around Qnity’s ability to sustain its margin profile and return on equity while carrying its new post-separation capital structure, particularly with a relatively new board still bedding down oversight. This leadership transition now sits squarely inside that risk discussion.

Qnity Electronics' shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$56 to US$105 per share, showing how differently individual investors are modelling Qnity’s prospects. Set against a recent leadership change in the finance function and a still-fresh spin-off track record, this spread highlights why you may want to weigh multiple viewpoints before forming a view on how reliably Qnity can execute on its current guidance.

data.title: Will APA Group's (ASX:APA) 1H26 Briefing and Lapsed Performance Rights Recast Its Incentive Narrative?
data.description:
• APA Group has scheduled its 1H26 financial results release and investor webcast briefing for 19 February 2026, led by CEO Adam Watson and CFO Garrick Rollason to update the market on performance and business positioning in Australia’s energy infrastructure sector.
  • Separately, the lapse of 29,529 performance rights after unmet vesting conditions subtly reshapes APA’s capital structure and may prompt closer scrutiny of how management incentives align with operational outcomes.

The upcoming 1H26 result and webcast remain the key short term catalyst, with any commentary on funding capacity and balance sheet strength especially important. The lapse of 29,529 performance rights is not material to that near term story, though it may focus attention on execution against internal hurdles.

The recent confirmation of an interim distribution of 27.5 cents per security for the six months to 31 December 2025 is the most relevant announcement to view alongside the 1H26 briefing. With profit margins compressed and interest costs not well covered by earnings, investors may pay close attention to how management explains the sustainability of current payout settings when they update the market on 19 February.

data.title: How Xometry’s New Materials and Controls Suite May Reshape Xometry (XMTR) Investors’ Risk Calculus
data.description:
• In early January 2026, Xometry announced a suite of platform enhancements, including eight new high-performance additive manufacturing materials, expanded CNC machining controls, looser tolerance options that can lower pricing, and CMMC Level 2–aligned sourcing tools aimed at aerospace, defense, medical, and automotive customers.
  • The combination of higher-spec materials, more granular control over manufacturing methods and geography, and security-focused sourcing positions Xometry as a more attractive partner for engineers and procurement teams with complex compliance and technical requirements.

To own Xometry, you need to believe its AI powered marketplace can convert growing digital manufacturing adoption into durable, profitable scale, despite ongoing losses and intense competition. The January 2026 platform upgrades mainly reinforce existing growth drivers in high complexity, regulated industries and do not materially change the near term catalyst of improving marketplace economics or the key risk around achieving sustainable GAAP profitability without further dilution.

The most relevant update is the expanded high performance materials lineup, including aerospace and medical grade polymers integrated into the Instant Quoting Engine. This directly supports the thesis that deeper technical capability and tighter manufacturing controls can attract higher value buyers and projects, which ties back to the catalyst of stronger enterprise adoption and increased revenue per customer if these capabilities see meaningful uptake.

data.title: How Euronext’s On-Chain Repo Trials (ENXTPA:ENX) Are Quietly Reframing Its Market Infrastructure Ambitions
data.description:
• A consortium of market infrastructure and trading firms recently completed a third wave of transactions on the Canton Network, using tokenized commercial bank deposits via LSEG Digital Settlement House and tokenized collateral such as European government bonds and U.S. Treasuries for on-chain intraday repo across multiple currencies and borders.
  • Euronext highlighted that this work is part of a broader industry push to build tokenization and on-chain infrastructure while staying aligned with regulated market requirements, underscoring how exchange operators are testing new settlement rails without stepping outside existing rulebooks.
  • We’ll now examine how Euronext’s on-chain repo participation and tokenization focus may influence its existing investment narrative and outlook.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

Euronext Investment Narrative Recap

To own Euronext, you need to believe it can keep compounding earnings by broadening beyond pure trading volumes while managing integration and regulatory headwinds. The Canton Network on-chain repo tests support this diversification angle but do not materially change the near term focus on volume normalization risk and the share buyback plus potential CAC 40 inclusion as key sentiment drivers.

The most relevant recent development is Euronext’s ongoing acquisition of the Athens Stock Exchange (ATHEX), which expands its clearing, settlement and listing footprint. Taken together with its tokenization experiments, this points investors toward a broader market infrastructure story where execution risk around integration and returns on new platforms sits alongside the potential uplift from index inclusion and capital returns.

Yet while these initiatives look promising, investors should be aware that revenue tied to trading activity could still...

data.title: The Bull Case For RemeGen (SEHK:9995) Could Change Following AbbVie’s RC148 Licensing Deal - Learn Why
data.description:
• On 12 January 2026, AbbVie and RemeGen announced an exclusive licensing agreement for RC148, a PD-1/VEGF-targeted bispecific antibody, giving AbbVie rights outside Greater China in exchange for a US$650 million upfront payment plus very large potential milestone payments and tiered, double-digit royalties.
  • This deal not only validates RemeGen’s immuno-oncology platform, but also ties its future RC148 opportunity to AbbVie’s global ADC and solid tumor portfolio.
  • We’ll now examine how this extensive AbbVie partnership around RC148 could reshape RemeGen’s investment narrative and long-term oncology positioning.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

What Is RemeGen's Investment Narrative?

To own RemeGen today, you really have to believe in its ability to turn a broad, loss‑making pipeline into durable, commercial oncology and autoimmune franchises. The AbbVie deal around RC148 is a clear swing factor in that story: the US$650 million upfront meaningfully extends the cash runway and reduces balance sheet risk in the near term, while shifting one of RemeGen’s key solid‑tumor assets into a global partnership model. In the short run, the main catalysts now cluster around RC148’s development progress with AbbVie and late‑stage readouts and filings for telitacicept and DV, rather than any buyback activity, which has been a non‑event so far. The flip side is that execution risk, high valuation multiples and dependence on a few lead programs become even more central to the equity case.

However, one business risk in particular is easy to underestimate at first glance. RemeGen's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 14%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Simply Wall St Community members currently offer 1 fair value view around HK$114.07 for RemeGen, showing how even a single detailed model can differ from market pricing. Set against the AbbVie RC148 deal and RemeGen’s still‑meaningful losses, that community input underlines how different investors can weigh partnership upside versus execution and concentration risks, which you should consider alongside professional estimates.

data.title: Silver Storm Mining (TSXV:SVRS) Valuation After La Parrilla Drilling Kickoff And OTCQX Upgrade
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Silver Storm Mining is on investors’ radar right now

Silver Storm Mining (TSXV:SVRS) has caught fresh attention after starting underground drilling at its La Parrilla Silver Mine Complex in Mexico and qualifying to trade on the OTCQX Best Market in the U.S.

See our latest analysis for Silver Storm Mining.

The recent commencement of underground drilling at La Parrilla and the move to the OTCQX market appear to be coinciding with strong momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 29.41%, a 90 day share price return of 115.69%, and a very large 1 year total shareholder return off a low base.

If you are looking beyond precious metals, this could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership as potential next ideas.

With CA$0 revenue, a recent loss of CA$15.20 million and the share price now around CA$0.55, which is in line with its analyst target, you have to ask: is there still a buying opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Price-to-Book of 8.2x: Is it justified?

Silver Storm Mining is trading on a P/B of 8.2x, which sits above the Canadian Metals and Mining industry average of 3.4x and roughly in line with peers at 13.2x.

P/B compares the company’s market value to its book value. It is particularly watched for asset heavy businesses such as miners where the balance sheet and resource base are central.

For Silver Storm Mining, this 8.2x P/B comes alongside no meaningful revenue, an unprofitable track record and a return on equity of 28.96% in loss terms. The current ratio therefore suggests the market is placing a premium value on its assets and potential rather than recent financial results.

Against the wider Canadian Metals and Mining industry, the company trades at more than double the industry average P/B of 3.4x. However, its 8.2x ratio is well below the peer group average of 13.2x, which suggests the market is pricing it at a discount to similar names but at a clear premium to the broader sector.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-book of 8.2x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, you are still dealing with a CA$15.20 million loss and no current revenue, so any setback at La Parrilla or funding pressure could quickly test sentiment.

data.title: Earnings Tell The Story For Codan Limited (ASX:CDA) As Its Stock Soars 28%
data.description:
Codan Limited (ASX:CDA) shareholders would be excited to see that the share price has had a great month, posting a 28% gain and recovering from prior weakness. The annual gain comes to 139% following the latest surge, making investors sit up and take notice.

Following the firm bounce in price, Codan's price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 65.3x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in Australia, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 22x and even P/E's below 13x are quite common. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the highly elevated P/E.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Codan certainly has been doing a good job lately as it's been growing earnings more than most other companies. The P/E is probably high because investors think this strong earnings performance will continue. If not, then existing shareholders might be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

View our latest analysis for Codan

Keen to find out how analysts think Codan's future stacks up against the industry? In that case, our free report is a great place to start.
Advertisement

What Are Growth Metrics Telling Us About The High P/E?

In order to justify its P/E ratio, Codan would need to produce outstanding growth well in excess of the market.

If we review the last year of earnings growth, the company posted a terrific increase of 27%. However, the latest three year period hasn't been as great in aggregate as it didn't manage to provide any growth at all. So it appears to us that the company has had a mixed result in terms of growing earnings over that time.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 23% per annum during the coming three years according to the eight analysts following the company. With the market only predicted to deliver 17% each year, the company is positioned for a stronger earnings result.

In light of this, it's understandable that Codan's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. Apparently shareholders aren't keen to offload something that is potentially eyeing a more prosperous future.

The Bottom Line On Codan's P/E

The strong share price surge has got Codan's P/E rushing to great heights as well. We'd say the price-to-earnings ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

We've established that Codan maintains its high P/E on the strength of its forecast growth being higher than the wider market, as expected. At this stage investors feel the potential for a deterioration in earnings isn't great enough to justify a lower P/E ratio. Unless these conditions change, they will continue to provide strong support to the share price.

The company's balance sheet is another key area for risk analysis. Our free balance sheet analysis for Codan with six simple checks will allow you to discover any risks that could be an issue.

It's important to make sure you look for a great company, not just the first idea you come across. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies with strong recent earnings growth (and a low P/E).

data.title: Is Figure (FIGR) Quietly Rewriting Its Competitive Edge With Blockchain-Native Equity Trading?
data.description:
• In recent days, Figure Technology Solutions launched its On-Chain Public Equity Network (OPEN), a blockchain-based platform that enables companies to issue, trade, and settle real equity directly on the Provenance blockchain while bypassing traditional intermediaries.
  • This move highlights Figure’s push to make tokenized real-world equity more accessible, including peer-to-peer share lending and direct transfers that aim to cut friction in conventional market infrastructure.
  • With this backdrop, we’ll explore how OPEN’s blockchain-native equity issuance and trading capabilities shape Figure’s broader investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 108 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Figure Technology Solutions' Investment Narrative?

To own Figure Technology Solutions, you have to believe in its pitch as a blockchain-native capital markets infrastructure company, not just a consumer lender. The launch of OPEN sits squarely in that thesis, effectively turning Provenance into a venue for issuing and trading real equity while Figure’s own Nasdaq stock becomes the first test case. In the near term, the main catalysts still look tied to execution in its consumer loan marketplace and traction across its blockchain lending and trading platforms, but OPEN adds a new, potentially meaningful leg to the story if companies and liquidity providers actually adopt it at scale. The recent share price surge and rich sales multiple suggest expectations have already risen, which also heightens execution risk around a young management team, complex new products and very high growth assumptions already embedded in the stock.

However, there is one execution risk here that current shareholders cannot ignore. Figure Technology Solutions' shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates span from about US$15 to a very large upper bound, underlining how far apart views on Figure sit. Against that backdrop, OPEN’s success or disappointment, alongside already elevated expectations in the share price, could be a key swing factor for whether the business grows into this valuation or not, so it is worth weighing these contrasting perspectives carefully.

data.title: A Look At Northern Oil And Gas (NOG) Valuation As Dividend Yield And Well Expansion Raise Questions
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Northern Oil and Gas is on income investors’ radar

Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) is drawing attention as investors weigh its sizable dividend against a growth plan centered on expanding well ownership, a combination that has created short term pressure on free cash flow.

This mix of high current income, rising production interests, and questions about dividend sustainability is prompting closer scrutiny of the stock, especially as energy sector uncertainty keeps risk and reward tightly linked.

See our latest analysis for Northern Oil and Gas.

At a share price of $22.56, Northern Oil and Gas has seen a 7 day share price return of 4.06% and a 90 day share price return of 4.49%. Its 1 year total shareholder return is a 42.64% loss and its 5 year total shareholder return is a 152.38% gain, which suggests recent momentum is modest compared with a much stronger long term record.

If this mix of income and energy exposure has your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search with aerospace and defense stocks as another way to uncover differentiated cash flow stories.

With an 8.2% dividend yield, an intrinsic value estimate that sits above the current US$22.56 share price, and recent weak 1 year returns, you have to ask: is this a mispriced income play, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 26.5% Undervalued

The most followed narrative places Northern Oil and Gas' fair value at US$30.70, above the recent US$22.56 share price. This puts the spotlight firmly on the assumptions behind that gap.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $33.7 for Northern Oil and Gas based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the most bullish reporting a price target of $50.0, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just $25.0.

Read the complete narrative.

Revenue growth that is modest on paper, compressing margins, and a higher future earnings multiple all sit at the core of this valuation story. Curious which assumptions matter most for that US$30.70 fair value and how a 7.88% discount rate and share count changes tie together in these forecasts? The full narrative lays out the earnings path that needs to materialise for this pricing gap to close.

Result: Fair Value of $30.70 (UNDERVALUED)

However, shorter term cash flow pressure from weaker gas and NGL realizations, along with reliance on acquisitions for growth, could easily challenge that undervaluation story.

data.title: What Savara (SVRA)'s Exclusive PANTHERx MOLBREEVI Distribution Deal Means For Shareholders
data.description:
• PANTHERx Rare Pharmacy and Savara Inc. recently announced an exclusive U.S. distribution agreement for MOLBREEVI, following Savara’s December 2025 Biologics License Application submission to the FDA for treating autoimmune PAP with a Priority Review request.
  • This tie-up with a rare-disease focused pharmacy positions Savara with a specialized channel for potential commercialization, including clinical support, education, and financial assistance for patients and prescribers.
  • We’ll now examine how this exclusive rare-disease distribution partnership with PANTHERx shapes Savara’s investment narrative and commercial readiness for MOLBREEVI.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 22 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

What Is Savara's Investment Narrative?

To own Savara today, you really have to believe that MOLBREEVI can transition from a development asset into a commercial rare‑disease franchise, while the company funds its way through continuing losses and zero revenue. The resubmitted BLA with Priority Review requested already put regulatory timing at the center of the story; the new PANTHERx exclusive distribution deal now adds a clearer path to launch execution and patient access if approval comes through. In the near term, the biggest catalysts still cluster around the FDA’s response to the BLA and any updates on manufacturing or labeling, but this partnership modestly strengthens the commercialization side of that equation and may ease concerns about launch readiness. The flip side is unchanged: Savara is burning cash, recently diluted shareholders, and remains exposed if the BLA process hits further delays or setbacks.

However, investors should also consider how recent dilution and legal overhang might pressure future returns. Our valuation report here indicates Savara may be overvalued.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span a wide range, from just above US$1 to above US$10 per share, underscoring how differently people see Savara’s prospects. When you set that against the binary risk around MOLBREEVI’s BLA and the company’s ongoing losses, it highlights why examining several viewpoints before forming your own stance on the stock can be so important.
